issues.title: fix(text): player-facing copy audit -- traps fixed + hardcoded->variable
issues.body:
Audit of player-visible strings across `godot/scenes/**/*.tscn`, `godot/scripts/**/*.gd`, and `godot/data/**/*.json`. Full findings in `docs/game-design/TEXT_AUDIT_2026-07-20.md` (grouped by TRAPS vs HARDCODED-SHOULD-BE-VARIABLE, ranked by player-confusion risk).

## Applied (safe, low-risk)
- **H1** `config_confirmation.gd:52` -- starting-funding display now reads `Balance.num("starting_resources.money", 245000.0)` (the value `game_state.gd:217` actually boots with) instead of a hardcoded `245000` that silently drifts if the balance is retuned.

## Flagged for Pip (wording/design/behavioural calls -- NOT auto-edited)
- **T1 (highest risk)** `main.tscn:128` Papers tooltip: "reduce doom (-3 each)" is stale -- `actions.gd:341` prices that doom reduction at **0** under the ADR-0015 stream model. The "+5 reputation each" half is correct. Needs a design call on replacement copy.
- **T2** `main.tscn:338` "Hire SR" (`TestActionButton`) -- a dev/test quick-hire button enabled in the live control bar (`main_ui.gd:837`).
- **T3** `main.tscn:332` "Init" -- ambiguous dev-flavoured control-button label.
- **T4** `pregame_setup.tscn:285` + `config_confirmation.tscn:241` ">> INITIALIZE LAB" -- off-theme/kerning (matches the earlier "INITIALISE LAB" flag), two copies.
- **T5** `whats_new_modal.gd:202` fallback points players at `CHANGELOG.md` (repo file).
- **H2** balance literals baked into `main.tscn` tooltips (compute/paper/AP) -- correct today but drift-prone; making them data-driven needs new plumbing (tscn can't call `Balance`).

## Verification
Fast gate: **510 tests, 0 failures** (`run_godot_tests.py --quick --ci-mode --min-tests 300`). No GDScript parse errors.

Do NOT merge -- copy/design decisions are Pip's.

Traps found: 6 (fixed 0 -- all judgment calls). Hardcoded-should-be-variable found: 3 groups (fixed 1).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Perf: turn-1 / early-days processing feels slow (watch-and-track)
issues.body:
From Pip's v0.11.0 re-test (2026-07-20): "it also seemed to take a while on the first few days inside the turn cycle on the first turn -- maybe something to keep an eye on."

Early-game (turn 1 / first few day-ticks) has a noticeable processing pause. Not blocking, but worth a profiling pass: likely one-time setup work (event deck load, rival-lab init, baseline schedule, RNG warm-up) happening synchronously on the first turn. Options once profiled: defer/async the heavy init, spread it across the pre-game load, or surface it as a brief loading moment (ties into the loading-screen/stats-display idea + the accumulation vision doc #712).

Track alongside the leaderboard open-perf work (fix/leaderboard-open-perf-and-clear-label). Log timings when convenient.

issues.title: docs(vision): accumulation + evolution -- run telemetry as the game's self-building substrate
issues.body:
## What

Adds `docs/game-design/VISION_ACCUMULATION_AND_EVOLUTION.md` -- a living vision doc capturing Pip's north-star framing for what P(Doom)1's data *becomes* over time, then bolts architectural structure onto it.

Pip's words are preserved verbatim (core attraction, "things accumulate ... like runs on pdoom1", the aggregate-and-visualise ask, the self-building motif, the tree-of-life / Cambrian-explosion image). The coordinator's synthesis is layered on top:

1. **Run telemetry as the substrate** -- the leaderboard is the first instance of a run-telemetry system, not a scoreboard. `VerificationTracker`'s chained SHA-256 hash is the run's DNA; the `pdoom1-replay-v1` artifact (ADR-0006) is its shape.
2. **Contract-safe enrichment path** -- optionally carry the run's shape (input log + strategy signature + hash) as a *sibling telemetry channel*. The frozen score contract (ADR-0002) stays backward-compatible; enrichment is additive and ignorable.
3. **The visualisations it unlocks** (named as future work, not built): strategy-fingerprint histograms, a forking-channels strategy tree, deep-run replays, population-level exploit-discovery detection.
4. **The self-referential motif** -- the accumulating run-data and the accumulating ADR/change-history are the same shape of story; cross-links `DESIGN_PHILOSOPHY.md` and the `pdoom-data` A/B/C/D tiers as the sibling data plane.
5. **Near-term hooks** -- small do-now capture moves so the substrate accretes before any viz exists; keep ALL of it (explicit no-cleanup constraint).

Clearly separates **the dream** (aspirational, unbuilt) from **buildable next hooks**. Does NOT propose changing the frozen score contract.

## Note

This is Pip's own design philosophy -- **left for Pip to review and merge himself**, not for agent admin-merge.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: UI consistency sweep after asset promotion + office-sim integration (level-2 visual pass)
issues.body:
Pip's forward-looking concern (2026-07-19), captured so it isn't lost.

## The two levels of the art pipeline
- **Level 1 (building now):** the review app's Keep/Reroll wiring PROMOTES asset files into `godot/assets/` and feeds Rerolls into the next generation wave. Makes assets *available*.
- **Level 2 (this issue):** the game UI must actually *reference and render* the promoted assets, the office simulator gets folded onto a real game screen, and then -- crucially -- a CONSISTENCY SWEEP over ALL UI elements.

## The sweep
Once the new assets are promoted + the office-sim is integrated + round-2 UI improvements land, audit every UI element/screen for visual coherence against the locked style: warm-grime + heavy-outline heft + deeper shadow; palette from `docs/art/palette.json`; consistent icon stroke/sizing; panel-frame treatment; spacing; the doom-as-additive-layer intensity rules. Goal: old and new assets don't clash; the whole game reads as one visual system.

## Sequencing (blocked-until)
Do the sweep AFTER: (a) assets promoted (level 1), (b) office-sim folded into a screen (see #663 + the OfficeFloor integration task), (c) round-2 UI improvements. Approach: inventory all UI elements -> map each to current vs intended asset -> flag inconsistencies -> apply the style guide uniformly.

Not blocking the v0.11.0 alpha release (which ships with current UI). This is the visual-coherence capstone for the art push.

issues.title: Playtest v0.11.0: defeat-pacing drag + hiring pipeline feels gappy (coalesce/de-branch)
issues.body:
From Pip's live v0.11.0 playtest (2026-07-18). Post-alpha design work; NOT release-blocking (the end-game quit is separate and release-blocking -- see branch fix/endgame-quit-score-post).

## 1. Defeat pacing is a drag
Getting defeated took too long / felt low-stakes. Complements the existing "no loss in first ~6 turns" heuristic: the difficulty curve needs a genuine FAILURE RAMP after the safe early window, so the mid/late game has escalating stakes instead of a slow grind to the end. Tune the doom/finance/reputation pressure so a losing run resolves in a satisfying arc, not a war of attrition.

## 2. Hire -> onboard -> equip -> mentor pipeline feels incomplete
The pipeline doesn't read as fully there yet -- "a few little gaps in logics", and the onboard/equip/mentor stages don't land clearly. Pip's framing: "more coalescing and de-branching needs to happen" -- consolidate overlapping/parallel paths, and cut or finish half-wired branches so the flow is one coherent legible sequence rather than several partial ones.

Overall dev read: "internally about where I expect it to be" + "great progress" -- these are refinement items, not a teardown.

issues.title: Local Leaderboard.add_score does not dedupe by entry_uuid (remote PHP does)
issues.body:
Surfaced by the property tests in #699 (`test_property_reentry_rank_stable`).

## Finding
`godot/scripts/leaderboard.gd::add_score` unconditionally `entries.append(entry)` -- there is **no `entry_uuid` dedup**. The only `entry_uuid` logic is the rank-lookup loop. So:
- Adding the same `entry_uuid` twice **appends a duplicate** (board grows until the cap trims it).
- At cap (50), a duplicate `entry_uuid` can occupy two slots and **evict a distinct legitimate entry**.

## Severity: LOW (local-only)
- The **remote** PHP endpoint (`server/leaderboard/score_api.php`) **does** dedupe by `entry_uuid` (verified live: a re-POST returns `duplicate:true`, no double-count). So the shared board friends see is safe.
- This affects only the **local in-game** leaderboard display, and only if the same `entry_uuid` is added twice (normally each game-over generates a fresh uuid, so it needs a double-add/retry path to trigger).

## Fix
Dedupe by `entry_uuid` in `add_score` (skip or replace when the uuid already exists), matching the remote behaviour and the field's implied uniqueness. Add a property test asserting size-stability on re-add once fixed.

Not release-blocking for the v0.11.0 friends & family alpha (remote board is correct); good fast-follow.

issues.title: art: sweep review 2026-07-17 (verdicts + rulings) + tabbed viewer
issues.body: ## What Persists Pip's 2026-07-17 pixel-art review (sweep + re-roll batches) as the durable source of truth, adds Claude's design rulings, and rebuilds the art-review viewer. ### Docs - **`docs/art/reviews/SWEEP_REVIEW_2026-07-17.md`** (new): Pip's verdicts VERBATIM under `## Keep` / `## Maybe` / `## Re-roll` / `## Other notes`, plus `## Style direction` + `## Winning styles`. Then `## Rulings (Claude)` (unifying principle "Doom is a layer, not a repaint"; style lock; 7 rulings on Pip's tagged questions) and `## Re-roll work queue` (HELD pending Pip go), grouped by corrected approach. - **`docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`**: adds the "Doom is a layer, not a repaint" core principle + a stub operational doom ladder table (per band: glow hex / glow amount / ambient grade / distortion). ### Tooling - **`tools/art_review/verdicts.json`**: seeds 255 verdicts (99 keep / 57 maybe / 99 re-roll) + 171 notes, mapped onto viewer cell ids. Every id verified against a real cell (0 unmatched, 0 skipped). Existing style-matrix picks + notes preserved. - **`tools/art_review/build.py`**: rebuilds `style_review.html` into a single-file **tabbed** viewer: - **Group-by toggle** `Style | Category` -- STYLE is primary (Pip iterates style over stable prompts); auto-falls back to Category when the dataset has only one style value. - **Status filter** `All / Pending / Keep / Maybe / Re-roll`, orthogonal, applies within the active tab. - **Lazy render**: only the active tab's cells hit the DOM. - **Relative-path ``** instead of base64 -> **144 KB** (was ~1.66 MB); scales to bigger sweeps. - Single shared `localStorage` + `verdicts.json` store (one HTML file). Keyboard nav (arrows, K/M/R, N/Enter, Esc) + markdown export preserved. `style_review.html` is regenerable (`python tools/art_review/build.py`) and intentionally NOT committed. ## Verify - `python tools/art_review/build.py` -> `cells=297 missing_img=0`, 143,914 bytes. - HTML parses; group/filter/tab markup present; embedded DATA + SEEDED JSON valid. - 4 files staged by explicit path; no `.import`/`.uid`/`style_review.html` churn. ## Note / regression The old per-matrix "winner radio" UI is gone (it was tied to the endless-scroll matrix layout); `picks` still round-trips through the store + export and the style TABS now serve the compare-styles purpose. Flag if you want winner-picking re-added as a per-style-tab control. Do NOT merge -- for Pip's review. 🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: feat(ui): OfficeFloor directional walk cycles (E/N/W) -- real per-direction walk anims
issues.body:
## What

Adds real per-direction walk animations for the OfficeFloor worker, replacing the static rotation-frame fallback that #662 left in place.

When a worker walks **left / up / down**, they now play a proper walk cycle facing that way, instead of a frozen facing frame. South keeps using the base `walking` clip.

This is the polish that finishes Pip's #662 feedback ("characters should turn and face the direction they walk").

## How

- `employee_sprite.gd::_update_facing_visual()` already prefers a `walking_<facing>` clip when it exists (the seam deliberately left in #662). This PR just supplies those clips -- **zero code change**, pure art + resource wiring.
- 18 new frames (6 each for east / north / west), pixellab.ai char `0a6a27ed` (ArtLoop base-128, locked style), `walking-6-frames` template per direction.
- `office_worker.tres` grows from 4 -> 7 clips (`walking_east`, `walking_north`, `walking_west` added at speed 8.0, looping, matching the base south walk).

## Test

- `test_office_floor_sprite_frames.gd` updated to guard the grown resource (7 clips; each directional walk = 6 frames, all loop, all textures valid).
- Fast gate green: **414 tests, 0 failures**.

## Review

Run the demo and watch a worker path left/up/down:
```
godot --path godot res://scenes/ui/office_floor/office_floor_demo.tscn
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: docs: player-facing factual fixes + voice-rewrite teardown for ship (Pip review)
issues.body:
Ship-hygiene pass over **player-facing** docs (README top priority). Two kinds of change, kept deliberately separate.

## The core truth (verified in code)
`game_state.gd check_win_lose()` (L518-523) only ever sets `victory=false`, on `doom>=100` or `reputation<=0`. There is **no `doom<=0` victory branch** and `victory` is never set `true` anywhere. **The game is unwinnable by design** (ADR-0002 / DQ-1 victory-removal, RESOLVED). All fixes below use the true thesis: **you can't win — you can only buy time; survival duration is the score; every run ends in loss; a good run beats your own baseline.** (An earlier revision of this PR wrongly described a doom→0 "apex victory," following stale prose in the ADR-0002 *document* — that has been purged everywhere; see the audit report's correction note.)

## Factual fixes — safe to merge
- **README** — removed the advertised "Survive 100 turns with P(Doom) at 0%" win condition AND the About-para "…whether P(Doom) reaches 0% or humanity faces extinction" win-lie → buy-time framing. Fixed dead `docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md` link.
- **STEAM_INTEGRATION_ROADMAP** — version `v0.10.5 → v0.11.0` (×2); purged both win-lies in the store copy (short description + gameplay bullet).
- **PLAYERGUIDE** — corrected false "Python/Pygame version" note; dropped dead `CONFIG_SYSTEM.md` link.
- **docs/README** (index) — removed two all-dead link sections (8 dead links) + literal `CLIPBOARD`/`MICROSCOPE` emoji-token garbage + dead Dev Tools link.
- **CONTRIBUTING** — removed dead `docs/CONTRIBUTORS.md` link.
- **QUICK_REFERENCE** — version `v0.4.1 → v0.11.0`.

12 factual fixes total. No `.import` churn; `settings.local.json` excluded.

## Voice teardowns — **your worklist, not done here**
`docs/qa/PLAYER_FACING_AUDIT_2026-07-16.md` is the primary deliverable: verdict table + per-doc skeletons (README pitch, PLAYERGUIDE full rewrite — it documents the *pygame-era* game, DEMO_GUIDE archive, Steam store copy, PRIVACY, CHANGELOG L1-wave entries).

## Findings for other lanes (NOT fixed here)
- **In-game HUD center string** `"Win: P(Doom) = 0% or beat baseline | Lose: P(Doom) = 100%"` is the same stale win-lie, live in the game — owned by the HUD/code lane.
- **ADR-0002 doc prose is itself stale** (claims a `doom<=0` code victory that doesn't exist) — reconcile to DQ-1.
- **Steam achievement table** ("First Victory: Win your first game", "Safety Champion: Win with 0% doom", …) is win-based and invalid for an unwinnable game — must be redesigned around survival milestones by the achievements lane (also references deleted legacy python).

**Note:** `AP → Attention` wording was deliberately **flagged, not rewritten** — AP still co-exists in the shipped build (L2/#613 not built yet).

**Please don't merge blind:** the factual half is safe; the teardown half is for you.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: docs: visual asset options menu + pipeline inventory (Pip pick-and-iterate)
issues.body:
## What

A **menu of dials** for visual assets so Pip can pick a direction and iterate fast in parallel with playtesting — plus a full pipeline + asset inventory. **Research/prep lane: doc only. No images generated, no keys used, no binary assets committed.** Do NOT merge — this is for Pip to pick from.

New file: `docs/art/VISUAL_ASSET_OPTIONS_2026-07-16.md`

## Key findings

- **Pipeline runs today.** `tools/assets/generate_images.py` (gpt-image-1.5 default; dormant Gemini "Nano Banana Pro" backend with reference-image consistency). Deps installed locally (openai 2.8.0, Pillow 12.0.0, pyyaml); dry-run verified. **Only gap: `OPENAI_API_KEY` unset**, and the default model id carries a `TODO: confirm exact id vs live docs` — do one throwaway image to confirm id + true cost before a paid batch.
- **Existing scoped ideas found (refresh, not reinvent):** `art_prompts/hero_banners.yaml` (3 scene banners, already on-register, still `pending`); `docs/assets/ASSET_GENERATION_BATCH.md` (126-asset list, but **stale cyan/blue palette** — needs refresh); `ICON_MAPPING_AUDIT.md` (8 icons still rendering as placeholders).
- **Top gaps ranked:** (1) researcher/staff portraits — pure text today, the XCOM/attachment lever; (2) event/response-window hero art; (3) title/menu hero + CRT frame; (4) 8 placeholder-killer icons; (5) doom-meter (mostly wire-up of existing unused art); (6) office background; (7) ledger/finance chrome (deprioritise — deadpan text is on-tone).
- **Recommended first batch (~$2.85, ~51 imgs):** 3 scoped hero banners as-is + 8 refreshed placeholder icons + 6 researcher-portrait pilot.

## Open decisions for Pip
Portrait style (flat CRT-dossier vs painterly XCOM) · matched cast (Gemini+ref) vs independent · confirm model id/cost · per-genre event banners now or later · CRT frame worth mocking. See §7.

issues.title: Check Godot port for team_player uncapped-stacking exploit + poaching rate (salvaged from pre-Godot notes)
issues.body:
**Salvaged from the pre-Godot PERSONNEL_BALANCING_NOTES.md during the 2026-07-16 archival sweep.** Two personnel-balance concerns from the pygame era — verify whether they survived into the Godot port, fix if present. For the QA/bug-shakedown phase, not a blocker.

1. **team_player uncapped-stacking (potential exploit).** Old notes flagged a team_player trait whose synergy bonus stacked without a cap — N team-players compounding into a runaway surplus. By our exploit definition (engine misbehaviour, not clever strategy — DESIGN_PHILOSOPHY), an uncapped stack is a bug to get ahead of. Action: grep Godot researcher/trait code for additive-per-teammate synergy without a cap; if present, cap it + add a test. If traits are not ported yet, note so it is designed-out (ties DQ-15).

2. **Poaching rate.** Old notes had rival poaching ~4%/turn; Pip's target is roughly once a year (ADR-0011 / DQ-22 aggressive-hiring). Reconcile the current cadence to the once-a-year intuition (re-denominate like the other per-tick artifacts calibration caught).

Both feed DQ-15 (researcher archetypes) and DQ-22 (world-shoots-back midgame).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Add visual highlight effect for newly available actions
issues.body:
## Summary
When new actions become available (unlocked by meeting requirements), they should have a visual highlight effect until the player interacts with them.

## Proposed Implementation
- Track which actions player has 'seen' or interacted with
- New/unseen actions get a subtle glow or pulsing border
- Effect disappears after:
  - Player hovers over the action, OR
  - Player uses the action, OR
  - Next turn starts (optional)

## Example Use Cases
- Player publishes first paper → 'Submit Paper' action unlocks → highlighted
- Player reaches 30 reputation → 'Strategic' submenu unlocks → highlighted
- New hire types become available after certain turn → highlighted

## Technical Considerations
- Need to track 'seen' actions in GameState or separate tracking
- Could use shader effect or animated border
- Should be subtle, not distracting

## Priority
Nice-to-have - helps player discovery of new options

issues.title: Add hero art/images to event popup options
issues.body:
## Summary
Event popup buttons should have small 'hero art' images to make choices more visually appealing and help players quickly understand options.

## Proposed Implementation
- Add optional 'icon' field to event option definitions
- Display small art/icon next to each choice button
- Could use existing icon system or add event-specific art

## Example
For burnout event:
- Emergency Intervention: image of manager talking with team
- Team Retreat: image of outdoor bonding activity  
- Pizza Party: image of pizza boxes 🍕
- Push Through: image of stressed workers

## Priority
Nice-to-have, not blocking. Improves visual polish and player experience.

issues.title: Research Quality System (Rushed/Standard/Thorough)
issues.body:
## Summary
Implement research quality toggle that affects research speed, technical debt, and doom.

**Extracted from**: #429 (which covered Tech Debt + Upgrades + Research Quality)

---

## Feature Description

Three quality levels for research actions:

### Rushed
- **Speed**: 2x research generation
- **Cost**: Accumulates technical debt (+2 per turn)
- **Doom**: +1 doom per turn
- **Use Case**: Desperate situations, final push

### Standard
- **Speed**: 1x research generation (baseline)
- **Cost**: No debt
- **Doom**: Baseline doom
- **Use Case**: Normal gameplay

### Thorough
- **Speed**: 0.5x research generation
- **Cost**: No debt
- **Doom**: -1 doom per turn (safer!)
- **Use Case**: Safety-focused builds, early game

---

## Implementation

### Code Changes

```gdscript
# In actions.gd or new research_quality.gd:
const RESEARCH_QUALITY = {
    "rushed": {
        "research_multiplier": 2.0,
        "debt_per_turn": 2,
        "doom_modifier": 1.0
    },
    "standard": {
        "research_multiplier": 1.0,
        "debt_per_turn": 0,
        "doom_modifier": 0.0
    },
    "thorough": {
        "research_multiplier": 0.5,
        "debt_per_turn": 0,
        "doom_modifier": -1.0
    }
}
```

### Files to Modify
- `godot/scripts/core/actions.gd` - Add quality parameter to research actions
- `godot/scripts/core/game_state.gd` - Track current research quality setting
- `godot/scripts/core/turn_manager.gd` - Apply quality modifiers during turn processing
- `godot/scenes/main.tscn` or UI - Add quality selector (radio buttons/dropdown)

### Integration Points
- Technical Debt System (already implemented) - rushed research adds debt
- Doom System (already implemented) - quality affects doom modifiers

---

## UI Requirements
- Radio buttons or dropdown on research actions
- Show trade-offs clearly (speed vs safety vs debt)
- Default: Standard
- Remember last selection

---

## Estimated Effort
1-2 hours implementation + 1 hour testing

---

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Players can select research quality level
- [ ] Research speed scales with quality selection
- [ ] Rushed research accumulates technical debt
- [ ] Thorough research reduces doom
- [ ] UI clearly shows trade-offs

issues.title: Comprehensive upgrades system overhaul
issues.body:
## Summary
The 'upgrades' section needs a comprehensive overhaul tied into useful office and operational expenses.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Current upgrades feel disconnected from actual gameplay needs.

## Proposed Categories
### Office & Facilities
- Workspace quality (desks, chairs, lighting)
- Kitchen and amenities
- Meeting rooms
- Security systems
- Location upgrade (move to better office)

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- Compute clusters
- Development tools
- Network infrastructure
- Backup systems

### Software & Systems
- Accounting software (see #475)
- Project management tools
- Communication platforms
- HR systems

### Research Tools
- Datasets
- Specialized hardware
- Cloud credits
- Academic subscriptions

## Mechanics
- Each upgrade has:
  - Upfront cost
  - Ongoing maintenance
  - Tangible gameplay benefit
  - Clear prerequisite chain

## Integration
- Tied to budget management
- Visible in office representation
- Affects researcher productivity/morale
- Strategic choices: what to prioritize?

## Related Systems
- Office outfitting (#467)
- Finance management (#475)
- Personnel system

## Priority
High - core gameplay loop improvement
